title: Yacht Rental in Dubai 2026: Costs, Boats & Tips
html:
Dubai's coastline offers a spectacular setting for yacht rental, with departures from Dubai Marina or Port Rashid. Expect to pay from 1,200 AED for a half-day motorboat to 15,000 AED for a crewed luxury yacht. Start your search on BoatHire24 to compare verified listings and secure your ideal vessel.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Dubai?

Prices start at 600 AED per person for a shared tour and rise to 20,000 AED for a premium crewed yacht. Costs vary by boat size, duration, and season. Below is a breakdown of typical price bands.

  • Shared tour (2-3 hours): from 600 AED per person
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours, up to 8 guests): from 1,200 AED
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours, up to 12 guests): from 5,000 AED
  • Luxury crewed yacht (full day, up to 20 guests): from 15,000 AED

For the most accurate and up-to-date pricing, hire a boat through BoatHire24's platform, where you can filter by your budget and preferences.

Where to set off in Dubai

Dubai offers several excellent departure points for yacht rental. Dubai Marina is the most popular, with its modern promenade and easy access to the Arabian Gulf. Port Rashid provides a more traditional harbour setting, close to the historic district. The Palm Jumeirah's crescent offers exclusive berths near the Atlantis hotel. For a laid-back vibe, consider launching from Al Fahidi Marine Station or the Jumeirah Fishing Harbour. These marinas put you within easy reach of iconic sights like Burj Al Arab and the Dubai coastline.

Sail from Dubai Marina to the World Islands, about 4 nautical miles offshore, for a unique perspective on this artificial archipelago. The marina itself is home to hundreds of boats, from sporty Axopars to luxurious Sunseekers.

Which boat to rent

  1. Motor yacht (e.g. Azimut, Sunseeker), ideal for a glamorous day out with up to 12 guests, from 3,500 AED for half a day. Consider the Azimut 58 for hire, a popular choice for its sleek lines and spacious deck.
  2. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon), stable and spacious, perfect for groups of up to 20 guests; full-day hire from 5,000 AED.
  3. Sports boat (e.g. Axopar), fast and fun for water sports, up to 8 guests; half-day from 1,800 AED.
  4. Traditional dhow, a cultural experience, often with dinner, for up to 20 guests; evening cruises from 800 AED per person.

What do you need to know about licences and seasons?

In Dubai, you typically do not need a licence if you hire a crewed boat or a skipper; for bareboat rental a valid licence is required. The operator will confirm exact requirements based on the boat and your experience.

BoatHire24 recommends booking your yacht rental well in advance for the high season from November to March, when the Shamal wind can bring choppy conditions in the afternoons, morning trips are often calmer.

In 2026, the high season runs from November through March, with pleasant temperatures and clear skies. The summer months from June to September are hotter but offer lower rates and smaller crowds.

How to book a yacht rental in Dubai

Booking on BoatHire24 is straightforward. Browse available boats, compare prices, and read reviews from previous renters. Once you find your preferred vessel, you can book directly through the platform using secure payment. Use the boat search to filter by date, capacity, and price range to find the perfect option. Each listing includes a detailed description, photos, and the operator's contact information.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Dubai or list your own.

At a glance

  • Price band: 600, 20,000 AED
  • Peak months: November to March
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full day
  • Capacity: up to 20 guests on most yachts
  • Currency: AED (UAE dirham)

Questions about boat rental in Dubai

Can I rent a yacht in Dubai for a day without a licence?

Yes, you can rent a crewed yacht or a boat with a captain without holding a licence.

What is included in the price of a boat rental in Dubai?

Most rentals include the boat, skipper, fuel, and basic safety equipment; catering and water sports gear are often extra.

Is it safe to rent a boat in Dubai for a family?

Yes, Dubai's waters are generally calm and well-regulated, and operators prioritise safety with life jackets and briefing.

Can I bring my own food and drinks on a yacht rental in Dubai?

Most operators allow you to bring your own food and drinks, but check the listing policy for any restrictions.

Whether you are a first-time renter or an experienced boater, Dubai offers some of the finest yacht rental experiences in the world. Start planning your trip now on BoatHire24, and if you own a boat, consider listing it on the platform to rent out your boat to other enthusiasts.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Dubai and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
